Suppliers.bad_error: You must provide a valid tax organization type. [ORGANIZATION_TYPE_LOOKUP_CODE]
Suppliers.rerun_note: Top-level object; ${PREFIX} on VENDOR_NAME and SEGMENT1 on all three rows, so the fixture reloads on any run without colliding. Only standard enumeration codes referenced (no discovered tokens).

SupplierAddresses.bad_error: A value is required. You must provide a value. [COUNTRY]

SupplierSites.load_path: loadAndImportData (chained ImportSupplierSites), read-only BIP verification against base and interface tables
SupplierSites.bad_error: You must provide a valid value for either the VENDOR_ID or the VENDOR_NAME. [VENDOR_NAME]; A value is required. You must provide a value. [VENDOR_ID]

SupplierSiteAssignments.bad_error: You must provide a valid value. [BUSINESS_UNIT_NAME]
SupplierSiteAssignments.rerun_note: An assignment's natural key is vendor_site_id + client BU with no free-text component the prefix can vary; only three valid US1 client BUs exist. A 2nd run creates no new good base rows (re-finds run 1's ASSIGNMENT_IDs; run 2 prefix 33327, load 9766214). One live run is authoritatively proven; sustained re-running is not possible. Bad row remains per-run (carries prefix).

SupplierContacts.load_path: loadAndImportData (loads interface AND chains Import Supplier Contacts), read-only BIP verification
SupplierContacts.bad_error: You must provide a valid value for either the VENDOR_ID or the VENDOR_NAME. [VENDOR_NAME]

Customers.load_path: ONE FBDI zip (seven HZ interface CSVs) via loadAndImportData, chains Import Bulk Customer Data (CDMAutoBulkImportJob) async HZ import batch; read-only BIP verification
Customers.rerun_note: Prefix stays on every new-record key and the batch id, so the fixture reloads on a fresh prefix. loadAndImportData returns SUCCEEDED while Import Bulk Customer Data processes the batch asynchronously; a single run may show pass:false while the batch is still PROCESSING (batch reached terminal COMPL_ERRORS in ~2-3 min).

PurchaseOrders.load_path: loadAndImportData under fin_impl (four CSVs), then imported by ImportSPOJob (Import Orders) submitted under calvin.roth; read-only BIP verification
PurchaseOrders.bad_error: VENDOR_SITE_CODE=ZZINVALIDSITE: The supplier site isn't valid. Verify that the site is active, has the purchasing purpose assigned, is associated with the procurement business unit, and has an active assignment for the requisitioning business unit.
PurchaseOrders.notes: POs created in DOCUMENT_STATUS=INCOMPLETE (functional-user SUBMIT draft); reaching the base table is the pass bar. Load runs as fin_impl (calvin.roth returns HTTP 401 on the SOAP call); ImportSPOJob submitted as calvin.roth (fin_impl cannot submit, FUN-720397).

APInvoices.load_path: loadAndImportData (chains Import Payables Invoices), read-only BIP verification

GLBalances.load_path: loadAndImportData (chains Journal Import / JournalImportLauncher); base proof is GL_INTERFACE STATUS='P' carrying assigned JE_HEADER_ID
GLBalances.base_table: GL_JE_HEADERS (proven via GL_INTERFACE STATUS='P' with base JE_HEADER_ID)
GLBalances.rerun_note: Dates derived from today (ACCOUNTING_DATE, DATE_CREATED = today; PERIOD_NAME = today MM-YY), so every re-run lands in the currently-open period with no hard-coded period. GROUP_ID/reference carry the fresh prefix.

GLBudgets.load_path: Two-step: loadAndImportData into GL_BUDGET_INTERFACE, then one standalone ValidateAndLoadBudgets ESS submission per distinct Run Name; read-only BIP verification
GLBudgets.terminal_status: SUCCEEDED (load + good Run Name); ERROR (bad Run Name, expected)
GLBudgets.bad_error: You must specify a valid budget name.
GLBudgets.rerun_note: Prefix stays on the two Run Names (RT-GLBUD-G / RT-GLBUD-B) and inside the bad budget name, so it reloads on a fresh prefix. The accounting period is not hard-coded; a minimal GL_OPEN_PERIOD discovery returns the open period containing today. GOOD run 9766211 -> SUCCEEDED; BAD run 9766217 -> ERROR (expected).

Assets.bad_error: FLEX-VALUE DOES NOT EXIST {VALUESET}=FA_MAJOR_CATEGORY {SEGMENT}=Major Category {VALUE}=ZZINVALIDCAT You must enter a valid category combination. The ADDITION transaction type for ID 653201 couldn't be completed for asset number 19666RT-ASSET-BAD1.
Assets.rerun_note: PASS, but own-prefix (19666) direct base read was pending FA BIP replica refresh (returned 0 at report time); base bar met by the ESS PrepareMassAdditions log for this prefix plus the identical fixture already in FA_ADDITIONS_B on prior prefixes. NOTE: the good_base_ids listed are from prior prefixes (10063/10062/10057), not run 1's own prefix. ASSET_NUMBER/MASS_ADDITION_ID/DESCRIPTION carry ${PREFIX} so it reloads.

Requisitions.load_path: loadAndImportData under fin_impl (single SOAP call loads three interface tables and chains Import Requisitions / RequisitionImportJob); read-only BIP verification; no separate downstream job
Requisitions.bad_error: UOM_CODE=ZZZ: The UOM isn't valid. Verify that the UOM is active. When the UOM isn't the primary UOM of the item in the inventory organization, there must be an active standard UOM conversion or interclass UOM conversion for the provided UOM.
Requisitions.notes: Requisition created as an unsubmitted draft (DOCUMENT_STATUS=INCOMPLETE); reaching the base table is the pass bar. calvin.roth returns HTTP 401 on the SOAP service, so the call runs as fin_impl.

Projects.load_path: loadAndImportData under fin_impl (three CSVs) with chained Import Projects job (ImportProjectJobDef), ParameterList ',,Y'; read-only BIP verification; no separate downstream submitESSJobRequest
Projects.bad_error: IMPORT_STATUS=FAILURE LOAD_STATUS=COMPLETE (invalid source template; absent from PJF_PROJECTS_ALL_VL)
Projects.notes: Bad-row authoritative error text lives only in the ImportProjectReportJob output; the pass/fail decision uses the base-table read. bad_error here is the interface status from the evidence table.

BillingEvents.load_path: One FBDI zip (one CSV PjbBillingEventsXface.csv), one loadAndImportData call chaining ImportBillingEventJob; read-only BIP verification
BillingEvents.bad_error: 15949RT-BE-BAD1 did not reach PJB_BILLING_EVENTS (interface purged; rejection proven by base-absence while both good rows from the same load reached base)
BillingEvents.notes: Bad-row proof is absence from the base table (recipe sets bad_proof_is_absence:true) because PJB_BILLING_EVENTS_INT is unconditionally purged after ImportBillingEventJob finishes.

Expenditures.load_path: loadAndImportData stages PjcTxnXfaceStageAll.csv into PJC_TXN_XFACE_STAGE_ALL; separate submitESSJobRequest for Import and Process Cost Transactions (ImportAndProcessTxnsJob) validates+costs to base
Expenditures.bad_error: rejected on invalid expenditure type ZZ-BAD-EXPTYPE-99, MESSAGE_NAME=PJC_EXP_TYPE_INVALID
Expenditures.rerun_note: Fresh prefix stamped on ORIG_TRANSACTION_REFERENCE and per-row unique BATCH_NAME; GL/item dates use today so rows land in an open period.

ProjectBudgets.load_path: loadAndImportData into PJO_PLAN_VERSIONS_XFACE; Fusion auto-spawns Import Project Budgets (ImportBudgetsInterfaceData) which creates base plan versions and emits BudgetsXfaceBIP report
ProjectBudgets.bad_error: PJO_XFACE_INVALID_FPT ("The financial plan type ... doesn't exist in Oracle Fusion Project Control")

seed_values.project_name: Research Innovation Center Upgrade - Equipment Procurement

ProjectBudgets.rerun_note: Fresh prefix on the three plan version names, SRC_BUDGET_LINE_REFERENCEs, and the bad row's invalid plan type, so it reloads without colliding. Bad-row ERROR proven by absence from base due to documented interface-replica lag.

Items.load_path: loadAndImportData uploads zip to UCM, runs Load File to Interface Tables, chains ItemImportJobDef; SCM object using scm_impl for both load and verify
Items.bad_error: You must provide a valid value for the attribute organization (PROCESS_STATUS=3 in the interface before purge)
Items.rerun_note: Only ${PREFIX} is a placeholder (item numbers pos 4/10, batch id pos 2) so re-runs never collide. SCM base replica lags ~2 min; re-run verify.py a couple minutes after load to see good ids in base.

BlanketPOs.load_path: loadAndImportData (fin_impl) uploads zip to UCM prc/blanketPurchaseAgreement/import; ImportBPAJob (Import Blanket Agreements) submitted under calvin.roth creates agreements in PO_HEADERS_ALL
BlanketPOs.bad_error: VENDOR_SITE_CODE=ZZINVALIDSITE: The supplier site isn't valid. Verify that the site is active, has the purchasing purpose assigned, is associated with the procurement business unit, and has an active assignment for the requisitioning business unit.
BlanketPOs.rerun_note: ${PREFIX} stays on natural keys only (agreement number, interface source keys, numeric header BATCH_ID pos 3, RT${PREFIX} import source, ParameterList args 5/6/8), so it reloads on a fresh prefix. Two-user orchestration: load as fin_impl, import as calvin.roth.

Contracts.load_path: loadAndImportData (fin_impl) uploads to UCM prc/contractPurchaseAgreement/import; ImportCPAJob (Import Contract Agreements) submitted under calvin.roth creates agreements in PO_HEADERS_ALL
Contracts.bad_error: VENDOR_SITE_CODE=ZZINVALIDSITE: The supplier site isn't valid. Verify that the site is active, has the purchasing purpose assigned, is associated with the procurement business unit, and has an active assignment for the requisitioning business unit.
Contracts.rerun_note: ${PREFIX} stays on natural keys only (agreement number, interface source key, numeric BATCH_ID, import-source label RT${PREFIX}, ParameterList batch label), so it reloads on a fresh prefix. Two-user orchestration: load as fin_impl, import as calvin.roth.

MiscReceipts.load_path: loadAndImportData (scm_impl) loads INV_TRANSACTIONS_INTERFACE at PROCESS_FLAG=1; downstream PollTMEssJob (Manage Inventory Transactions) posts valid rows to INV_MATERIAL_TXNS
MiscReceipts.rerun_note: ${PREFIX} stamps the natural key TRANSACTION_REFERENCE and batch SOURCE_HEADER_ID/SOURCE_LINE_ID; ${TXN_DATE} is today's timestamp so receipts land in an open inventory period. SCM object using scm_impl for both load and verify.

Banks.load_path: Cash Management REST resources under fin_impl: POST cashBanks -> cashBankBranches -> cashBankAccounts (each POSTed after parent returns 201)
Banks.terminal_status: 201 (per POST); load summary banks 2 ok / 1 err, branches 2 ok, accounts 1 ok
Banks.bad_error: The value of the attribute Country isn't valid.
Banks.rerun_note: ${PREFIX} stamps all bank/branch/account names and numbers; routing numbers computed from prefix. Fresh names every run, chain internal to fixture, so no dependency on prior load. Also loaded live at prefix 92271. No ESS/HDL request id (REST fixture); run via load_rest.py then verify_rest.py.

ValueSets.load_path: POST /fscmRestApi/resources/11.13.18.05/valueSets/FA_MAJOR_CATEGORY/child/values (standalone load_rest_vsv.py), then read-only BIP verify
ValueSets.rerun_note: Two consecutive runs (prefixes 94120, 94137) landed cleanly with no collision, no reset; new value codes carry the prefix (G<prefix>N). Credential role fin_impl.

Lookups.load_path: FSM setupTaskCSVImports task FND_MANAGE_STANDARD_LOOKUPS as fin_impl (run_lookups_fsm.py -> load_fsm_csv.py), poll ProcessCompletedFlag, read-only BIP verify
Lookups.terminal_status: Completed with warnings (3 code rows processed, bad row skipped)
Lookups.bad_error: Parent row is missing in file FND_APP_STANDARD_LOOKUP.csv for row 3 in file ORA_FND_APP_STANDARD_LOOKUP_CODE.csv. Row will be skipped
Lookups.rerun_note: Standalone reference data with no upstream reference to discover; only templated token is ${PREFIX} on the lookup type code (RT_GOLD_${PREFIX} / RT_NO_SUCH_TYPE_${PREFIX}), so it reloads cleanly. v2 is a byte-identical copy of v1. Good ids are natural keys.

UnitsOfMeasure.load_path: FSM 'Setup Data Import from CSV file' setupTaskCSVImports, task INV_MANAGE_UNITS_OF_MEASURE (run_uom_fsm.py -> load_fsm_csv.py), then read-only BIP verify
UnitsOfMeasure.terminal_status: Completed (2 processed, 1 failed = intended bad row)
UnitsOfMeasure.bad_error: UomClass does not exist; skipping record. (uom :GldRegUOM 19742 BAD,uomCode:8EC,uomClassCode:ZZ_NO_SUCH_CLASS) ,Skipping the row
UnitsOfMeasure.rerun_note: Two consecutive runs (19742, 71793) on same pod, no reset/cleanup, both passed; fresh prefix changes UOM name and derives a distinct 3-char code stem. Credential role scm_impl. No explicit SUCCEEDED terminal-status token stated.

PaymentTerms.load_path: FSM 'Setup Data Import from CSV file' setupTaskCSVImports, TaskCode AP_MANAGE_PAYMENT_TERMS (run_paymentterms_fsm.py -> load_fsm_csv.py), then read-only BIP verify
PaymentTerms.terminal_status: Completed (3 rows processed, 1 failed = intended bad row)
PaymentTerms.bad_error: JBO-27024: Failed to validate a row ... in PaymentTermSubscriptionEO / JBO-27014: Attribute SetId in PaymentTermSubscriptionEO is required
PaymentTerms.rerun_note: Two consecutive runs (83810, 97334) both loaded to base with no collision, no reset/cleanup; fresh prefix in term Name gives distinct names/TERM_IDs. Credential role fin_impl. No explicit SUCCEEDED terminal-status token.

Workers.load_path: HCM Data Loader (upload -> createFileDataSet -> poll) as hcm_impl; Worker.dat inside Workers_gold.zip
Workers.bad_error: You need to enter a valid value for the LegalEntityId attribute. The current values are DMT NONEXISTENT LEGAL EMPLOYER.
Workers.rerun_note: Top-level new-hire; each run creates brand-new prefix-stamped persons with run-unique SSN, so no stateful collision. Terminal ORA_IN_ERROR is expected (2 good loaded, 1 bad errored).

Salaries.load_path: HCM Data Loader (upload -> createFileDataSet -> poll), verified read-only against CMP_SALARY
Salaries.bad_error: You need to enter a valid value for the SalaryBasisId attribute...
Salaries.rerun_note: Two consecutive runs both passed. SAL_DATE is prefix-derived (2020-01-01 + prefix days), so each run inserts a NEW date-effective salary segment on the same seeded assignment instead of colliding. Run 2 (prefix 83145, dataset 9766135, ORA_IN_ERROR) produced salary_ids 300000331562342 (E10) / 300000331562345 (E12). ORA_IN_ERROR is expected (2 loaded, 1 errored).

SalaryBases.load_path: HCM Data Loader (hcm_impl): upload -> createFileDataSet (IMPORT_AND_LOAD) -> poll DataSetStatusCode; SalaryBasis.dat, three MERGE lines
SalaryBases.bad_error: You need to enter a valid value for the ElementTypeId attribute. The current values are DMT NONEXISTENT ELEMENT 45660,US Legislative Data Group.
SalaryBases.rerun_note: ${PREFIX} stays on SourceSystemId and SalaryBasisName so each run inserts uniquely-named salary bases and reloads cleanly. Terminal ORA_IN_ERROR is expected (2 good loaded, 1 bad errored).

PayrollRelationships.load_path: HCM Data Loader REST: uploadFile -> createFileDataSet (IMPORT_AND_LOAD) -> poll dataLoadDataSets (AssignedPayroll), then read-only BIP verify
PayrollRelationships.bad_error: You need to enter a valid value for the PayrollId attribute. The current values are DMT NONEXISTENT PAYROLL,300000046974970.
PayrollRelationships.rerun_note: Stateful case; re-run safe by putting prefix into the assigned payroll's date-effective key (StartDate = 2020-01-01 + prefix days). Two consecutive runs (67592 StartDate 2205-01-23, 19649 StartDate 2073-10-18) each inserted a new date-effective segment on same E15/E16, both coexist. Terminal ORA_IN_ERROR expected (2 loaded, 1 errored).

TaxCards.load_path: HCM Data Loader REST: uploadFile -> createFileDataSet (IMPORT_AND_LOAD) -> poll dataLoadDataSets (TaxWithholding / FederalTaxes / FederalTaxes2023), then read-only BIP verify
TaxCards.bad_error: You need to enter a valid value for the SourceId attribute. The current values are 64617DMT-NO-REL.
TaxCards.rerun_note: CREATE is one-shot per payroll relationship. Run 1 (64617) created dir_card_id 300000331574506; run 2 (95682) MERGE onto the SAME card (same dir_card_id, no new create) still passes but does NOT create a fresh card. A date-split to force a fresh dated component per run was rejected by HDL. Terminal ORA_IN_ERROR expected (1 loaded, 1 errored).

W2Balances.load_path: HCM Data Loader REST: uploadFile -> createFileDataSet (IMPORT_AND_LOAD) -> poll dataLoadDataSets (InitializeBalanceBatchHeader + InitializeBalanceBatchLine), then read-only BIP verify

request_ids.downstream: ESS 'Load Initial Balances' (documented, not in standalone load path)

W2Balances.bad_error: You need to enter a valid value for the BatchId attribute. The current values are 300000046974970,DMTW260133NOSUCH.
W2Balances.rerun_note: ${PREFIX} on batch name (DMTW2<prefix>) makes each run a brand-new batch. Two consecutive runs (60133, 41088), fresh BATCH_LINE_IDs, no collision. Use PAY_BAL_BATCH_* tables (not PAY_BALANCE_BATCH_*). request_ids.load is the UCM ContentId. Terminal ORA_IN_ERROR expected (load 3 ok / 1 err).

BenParticipant.load_path: HCM Data Loader (hcm_impl): upload -> createFileDataSet (IMPORT_AND_LOAD) -> poll; PersonBenefitBalance.dat MERGE lines with fixed SourceSystemId per good row
BenParticipant.bad_error: You need to enter a valid value for the BnftsBalId attribute. The current values are DMT NONEXISTENT BENEFIT BALANCE.
BenParticipant.rerun_note: Stateful case, proven re-run safe over two consecutive runs (run 1 prefix 46570 dataset 9766566; run 2 prefix 21722 dataset 9766582). Each good row owns a FIXED SourceSystemId (DMTBEN-SEED-13/19) so a second run MERGE-updates our own record instead of colliding; only Val carries ${PREFIX}. Uses wall-clock-monotonic ${BEN_DATE}. Terminal ORA_IN_ERROR expected.

BenDependent.load_path: HCM Data Loader REST: uploadFile -> createFileDataSet (IMPORT_AND_LOAD) -> poll dataLoadDataSets (Contact + children), then read-only BIP verify
BenDependent.bad_error: The ZZINVALID value for the ContactType attribute is invalid and doesn't exist in the CONTACT list of values.
BenDependent.rerun_note: Natural re-run safety; every dependent key carries ${PREFIX} so each run creates a fresh dependent person. Two consecutive runs (67254, 61478) both passed, distinct relationship ids, no collision. Terminal ORA_IN_ERROR expected (import 12 ok/0 err, load 2 ok/1 err).

BenBeneficiary.load_path: HCM Data Loader REST: uploadFile -> createFileDataSet (IMPORT_AND_LOAD) -> poll dataLoadDataSets, then read-only BIP verify
BenBeneficiary.bad_error: You need to enter a valid value for the BnftsBalId attribute. The current values are DMT NONEXISTENT BENEFICIARY BALANCE.
BenBeneficiary.rerun_note: Stateful case; re-run safe via FIXED SourceSystemId per good row. Two consecutive runs (40521, 72360); run 2 MERGE updated same PER_BNFTS_BAL_IDs with new prefix-stamped Val, no collision. Terminal ORA_IN_ERROR expected (2 loaded, 1 errored).

Absences.load_path: HCM Data Loader (hcm_impl): upload -> createFileDataSet -> poll; PersonAbsenceEntry.dat referencing existing seeded persons
Absences.bad_error: You need to enter a valid value for the AbsenceTypeId attribute.
Absences.rerun_note: Stateful case, proven re-run safe over two consecutive runs (run 1 prefix 16928 dataset 9766600, window 2026/12/28->12/29; run 2 prefix 31764 dataset 9766622, window 2027/05/10->05/11). Absence window is prefix-derived (today + 30..330 days, snapped forward to next Monday) so each run books a new distinct window on the same persons; ${PREFIX} also on SourceSystemId. Terminal ORA_IN_ERROR expected.

TalentProfiles.load_path: HCM Data Loader REST: uploadFile -> createFileDataSet (IMPORT_AND_LOAD) -> poll dataLoadDataSets (ProfileItem), then read-only BIP verify
TalentProfiles.bad_error: You need to enter a valid value for the ContentItemId attribute. The current values are 104,DMT NONEXISTENT CONTENT ITEM 31070.
TalentProfiles.rerun_note: Stateful case; re-run safe via FIXED SourceSystemId plus a non-self evaluator qualifier pair. Two consecutive runs (31070, 57770); run 2 MERGE updated same PROFILE_ITEM_IDs, no duplicate/collision. Terminal ORA_IN_ERROR expected (2 loaded, 1 errored).

Assignments.load_path: HCM Data Loader REST: uploadFile -> createFileDataSet (IMPORT_AND_LOAD) -> poll dataLoadDataSets (Worker.dat: WorkTerms + Assignment), then read-only BIP verify
Assignments.bad_error: You must enter a valid value for the ActionCode field.
Assignments.rerun_note: Stateful date-effective case; re-run safe via wall-clock-monotonic effective date ${BEN_DATE}. Two consecutive runs (30059, 39099); run 2 MERGE updated same assignment_ids at same-day split via user key, no collision. request_ids.load is the UCM ContentId; request_ids.dataset is the HDL RequestId. Terminal ORA_IN_ERROR expected (2 loaded, 1 errored).

PerformanceEvaluations.load_path: HCM Data Loader REST: uploadFile -> createFileDataSet (IMPORT_AND_LOAD) -> poll dataLoadDataSets (GoalPlan object), then read-only BIP verify
PerformanceEvaluations.bad_error: The DMT_INVALID_TYPE value for the GoalPlanTypeCode attribute is invalid and doesn't exist in the ORA_HRG_GOAL_PLAN_TYPE list of values.
PerformanceEvaluations.rerun_note: Each good row creates a brand-new goal plan keyed on ${PREFIX}; fresh prefix per run yields distinct names/ids. Two consecutive runs (29379, 42443) both passed, no collision. Terminal ORA_IN_ERROR expected (2 ok / 1 err).

WorkSchedules.load_path: HCM Data Loader REST: uploadFile -> createFileDataSet (IMPORT_AND_LOAD) -> poll dataLoadDataSets (WorkPattern + WorkPatternShift), then read-only BIP verify
WorkSchedules.bad_error: You need to enter a valid value for the ShiftName attribute. The current value is DMT NONEXISTENT SHIFT 34027.
WorkSchedules.rerun_note: Stateful case; re-run safe via prefix-derived ${SAL_DATE} DateFrom plus bounded single-day window (DateTo = DateFrom) so windows never overlap. Two consecutive runs (34027, 41657) on same E8/E9, new work-pattern ids, no collision. An earlier open-ended (no DateTo) attempt failed on re-run with 'Work patterns can't overlap'. Terminal ORA_IN_ERROR expected (2 ok / 1 err).
